questions.questionText: In a right-angled triangle, the angle is 40° and the hypotenuse is 15 cm. What is the length of the side opposite the angle, correct to 2 decimal places?
questions.explanation: Use the sine ratio: sin(θ) = Opposite / Hypotenuse. So, Opposite = Hypotenuse × sin(θ) = 15 × sin(40°) ≈ 9.64 cm.

questions.questionText: A right-angled triangle has an angle of 55° and the hypotenuse is 20 cm. Calculate the length of the adjacent side, correct to 2 decimal places.
questions.explanation: Use the cosine ratio: cos(θ) = Adjacent / Hypotenuse. So, Adjacent = Hypotenuse × cos(θ) = 20 × cos(55°) ≈ 11.47 cm.

questions.questionText: In a right-angled triangle, an angle is 32°. The side adjacent to this angle is 18 cm long. Find the length of the opposite side, correct to 2 decimal places.
questions.explanation: Use the tangent ratio: tan(θ) = Opposite / Adjacent. So, Opposite = Adjacent × tan(θ) = 18 × tan(32°) ≈ 11.25 cm.

questions.questionText: In a right-angled triangle, the side opposite an angle of 25° is 8 m long. What is the length of the hypotenuse, correct to 2 decimal places?
questions.explanation: Use the sine ratio: sin(θ) = Opposite / Hypotenuse. So, Hypotenuse = Opposite / sin(θ) = 8 / sin(25°) ≈ 18.93 m.

questions.questionText: In a right-angled triangle, the side opposite an angle θ is 10 cm and the side adjacent to it is 15 cm. Calculate the size of angle θ, correct to 1 decimal place.
questions.explanation: Use the tangent ratio: tan(θ) = Opposite / Adjacent. So, θ = arctan(Opposite / Adjacent) = arctan(10 / 15) ≈ 33.7°.

questions.questionText: A ladder of length 12 m leans against a vertical wall. Its foot is 5 m from the base of the wall on horizontal ground. What is the angle the ladder makes with the ground, correct to 1 decimal place?
questions.explanation: The ladder is the hypotenuse (12 m) and the distance from the wall is the adjacent side (5 m). Use the cosine ratio: cos(θ) = Adjacent / Hypotenuse. So, θ = arccos(5 / 12) ≈ 65.4°.

questions.questionText: From the top of a 50 m high cliff, the angle of depression to a boat at sea is 15°. How far is the boat from the base of the cliff, correct to the nearest metre?
questions.explanation: The angle of elevation from the boat to the cliff top is also 15°. The cliff height is the opposite side. Use the tangent ratio: tan(15°) = Opposite / Adjacent = 50 / distance. So, distance = 50 / tan(15°) ≈ 187 m.

questions.questionText: A vertical pole of height 8 m casts a shadow of length 12 m. What is the angle of elevation of the sun, correct to 1 decimal place?
questions.explanation: The pole is the opposite side (8 m) and the shadow is the adjacent side (12 m). Use the tangent ratio: tan(θ) = Opposite / Adjacent. So, θ = arctan(8 / 12) ≈ 33.7°.

questions.questionText: An aircraft flies 120 km on a bearing of 060°. How far east has it travelled from its starting point, correct to the nearest km?
questions.explanation: The bearing of 060° creates a 60° angle with the North line. The distance travelled east is the side opposite this angle in the right-angled triangle. Use sine: East = 120 × sin(60°) ≈ 104 km.

questions.questionText: In a right-angled triangle, the two shorter sides are 7 cm and 24 cm. What is the sine of the smallest angle?
questions.explanation: First, find the hypotenuse using Pythagoras' theorem: √(7² + 24²) = 25 cm. The smallest angle is opposite the shortest side (7 cm). The sine of an angle is Opposite / Hypotenuse. So, sin(θ) = 7 / 25.

questions.questionText: In a right-angled triangle PQR where the right angle is at Q, PQ = 10 cm and angle PRQ = 42°. Find the length of QR, correct to 2 decimal places.
questions.explanation: Angle R is 42°, the opposite side is PQ = 10 cm, and the adjacent side is QR. Use the tangent ratio: tan(42°) = Opposite / Adjacent = 10 / QR. Rearranging gives QR = 10 / tan(42°) ≈ 11.11 cm.

questions.questionText: In triangle ABC, angle B = 90°, AC = 18 cm, and BC = 11 cm. What is the size of angle BAC, correct to 1 decimal place?
questions.explanation: Angle BAC is the angle at vertex A. The opposite side is BC (11 cm) and the hypotenuse is AC (18 cm). Use the sine ratio: sin(A) = Opposite / Hypotenuse = 11 / 18. So, A = arcsin(11 / 18) ≈ 37.7°.
